title: Völundarkviða
stanzas: Long ago there dwelt three brothers fair,
In a land of valleys green and wide,
Völund was master of the craft so rare,
Of forging gold and silver bright with pride.
With Slagfid and Egil, his brothers twain,
They labored long at forge and flame,
And Slagfid wed a maiden from the south,
While Völund sought no wife nor shame.
One day there came to their forest home,
Three maidens fair with golden hair,
And Völund's heart was moved with love,
For Sigrün of the Valkyries so rare.
But her sisters took her back to the sky,
And left poor Völund alone to mourn,
For nine long years he waited and wept,
For his lost love on a winter's morn.
Then came to him the king of the north,
And bound him fast with a magic spell,
And took him prisoner to his hold,
Where he forged the treasures of god and elf.
But Völund was cunning and bided his time,
Until he had forged a plan complete,
He made a sword of unmatched might,
And wings of gold to bear his flight.
One night he rose on wings of gold,
And flew away from the tyrant's keep,
And sought out Sigrün in the distant sky,
Where the Valkyries rode both dark and deep.
And there she waited with arms open wide,
To welcome him to her home above,
And together they flew through the starry night,
In the joy of their love forever blessed.
